0x00.bit_splitting:
DESC=Type:HEX_MASK=0000FFFF:MIN=0:MAX=0xFFFF:NF=2:402=Electric Drive:401=Generic I/O:419=Battery charger:418=Battery module:410 = inclinometer
DESC=Additional data:HEX_MASK=FFFF0000:MIN=0:MAX=0xFFFF:NF=BASE16
0x00.description: Contains information about the device type. This actually describes profiles. One node can have more than one profile (e.g. 1 or 2 Drives and Motion Control profiles and an I/O Module profile). The device type at this location is for the first profile. The device type for each profile is stored at 67FFh + (x * 800h), where x is the profile number. The value indicates it is a multiple profile device (FFFF) and the 1st profile is drives and motion (402d = 192h).
0x00.name: Device type

0x00.bit_splitting:
DESC=Generic error:HEX_MASK=1:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Cleared:1=Set
DESC=Current error:HEX_MASK=2:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Cleared:1=Set
DESC=Voltage error:HEX_MASK=4:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Cleared:1=Set
DESC=Temperature error:HEX_MASK=8:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Cleared:1=Set
DESC=Comms error:HEX_MASK=10:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Cleared:1=Set
DESC=Device Profile error:HEX_MASK=20:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Cleared:1=Set
DESC=Manf specific error:HEX_MASK=80:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Cleared:1=Set
0x00.description:
Device error register. Gives details of the state of generic faults such as temperature, current, voltage, etc. Transmitted with the EMCY telegram.
Note reserved bit deliberately excluded from bitsplitting to simplify user interface
0x00.name: Error register

0x00.description: COB-ID and configuration of SYNC message.
0x00.name: Synchronization (SYNC) message COB-ID

0x00.description: Interval between SYNC messages in µs.
0x00.name: Interval between SYNC messages (Communication cycle period)

0x00.description: Length of the time window for synchronous PDOs in µs. This is the maximum time to transmit synchronous PDOs after receiving SYNC message.
0x00.name: Synchronous (SYNC) window length

0x00.description: Device name as a string. E.g.. Shiroko AC Controller. Device name will be stored in ROM.
0x00.name: Product name

0x00.description: Hardware version as a string.
0x00.name: Hardware version

0x00.description: Software version as a string. Version string will be stored in ROM.
0x00.name: Software version

0x00.description: Used for Life Guarding Protocol. Time is in ms. Refer to Node Guarding Protocol (section 9.2.6.2.2 in DS301). The default for the system is to use Heartbeats.
0x00.name: Guard time

0x00.description: Multiplied with the Guard Time to give the Life Time. Refer to Node Guarding Protocol (section 9.2.6.2.2 in DS301).
0x00.name: Life time factor

__meta__.description: This object must be written to store objects to non-volatile memory.
__meta__.name: Store command

0x01.description: Write 65766173h to this object to save to EEPROM. The number represents "save" (i.e. 'e' = 65h; 'v' = 76h; 'a' = 61h; 's' = 73h). Reading this object will return 1 meaning values in the Object Dictionary are only saved on command.
0x01.name: Save all parameters

__meta__.description: This object must be written to store objects in the range 1200h to 1FFFh to non-volatile memory. All other objects are stored autonomously.
__meta__.name: Restore command

0x01.description: Write 64616F6Ch to this object to load from EEPROM. The number represents "load" (i.e. 'd' = 64h; 'a' = 61h; 'o' = 6Fh; 'l' = 6Ch). Reading this object will return 1 meaning values in the Object Dictionary are restored.
0x01.name: Load all parameters

0x00.description: COB-ID of EMCY object. If zero COB-ID will equal 80h + Node ID
0x00.name: Emergency (EMCY) telegram COB-ID

0x00.description: Inhibit time for EMCY objects in 0.1ms / bit resolution.
0x00.name: EMCY Inhibit Time

__meta__.description:
Defines the expected heartbeat cycle time for each node. Must be higher than the corresponding producer heartbeat time. If the heartbeat time for a node is set to 0, then that node defaults to the Node Guarding protocol.
Format: 00NN TTTT h, where NN is the node ID and TTTT is the heartbeat time in 1ms/bit resolution. 75ms (4Bh) is the default time.
__meta__.name: Consumer heartbeat time

0x01.bit_splitting:
DESC=1st node- Heartbeat time:HEX_MASK=FFFF:MIN=0:MAX=0xFFFF:NF=BASE10:UNITS=ms
DESC=1st node- Node ID:HEX_MASK=FF0000:MIN=0:MAX=127:NF=BASE10:
0x01.name: Consumer heartbeat time for 1st node

0x02.name: Consumer heartbeat time for 2nd node

__meta__.description: General device information. Note that LSS requires every sub-index be given a value, and that, when all four sub-indices are appended to give the LSS address, they generate a unique identifier.
__meta__.name: Identity object

0x01.description: Sevcon's CiA CAN assigned vendor ID.
0x01.name: Vendor ID

0x02.description:
32-bit unsigned integer which can be used to identify the product. 
Format: RRvv VVAAh, where RR is the product range (eg 1 = Shiroko), vv is the variant (e.g. 1 = AC), VV is the voltage (eg 30h = 48V) and AA is the current (eg 46h = 700A).
0x02.name: Product code

0x03.description: Contains a CANopen revision number indicating the version of object dictionary for a given application build. In the format  RRRRVVVV where RRRR is the build version i.e. 0007 and VVVV indicates the version of the object dictionary for that build
0x03.name: CANopen (Object Dictionary) revision

0x04.description:
32 bit unsigned integer containing the product serial number. Currently, Sevcon serial numbers have the format YYYYMMNNNN, where YYYY is the year, MM is the month and NNNN is a unique integer. A 32 bit number can go up to 4292967296, which allows our preferred serial number format to fit.
Serial number will be stored in EEPROM.
0x04.name: Serial number

0x01.bit_splitting:
DESC=Baseline:HEX_MASK=1:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Inactive:1=Active
DESC=Driveability Select 1:HEX_MASK=2:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Inactive:1=Active
DESC=Driveability Select 2:HEX_MASK=4:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Inactive:1=Active
DESC=Low Speed:HEX_MASK=8:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=On:1=Off
DESC=BDI:HEX_MASK=10:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=On:1=Off
DESC=Service:HEX_MASK=20:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=On:1=Off
0x01.description:
Indicates if a driveability profile is active or not. Each bit corresponds to one profile. I.e.:
Bit 0: Baseline (should always be 1)
Bit 1: Driveability Select 1.
Bit 2: Driveability Select 2.
Bit 3: Low Speed
Bit 4: BDI
Bit 5: Service
0x01.name: Driveability Profile Status

0x02.description: State of main Traction Drive FSM
0x02.name: Traction Drive State

0x03.description: State of Drive/Direction Change FSM
0x03.name: Drive / Direction Change State

0x04.description: State of SRO FSM
0x04.name: SRO State

0x05.description: State of Operator Presence (Seat switch) FSM
0x05.name: Operator Presence (Seat) State

0x07.description: State of Bucyrus P-Start FSM
0x07.name: P-Start State (Bucyrus)

0x08.description: State of Bucyrus park brake FSM
0x08.name: P-Start State (Bucyrus)

0x09.description: State of Bucyrus direction selection FSM
0x09.name: Set Direction State (Bucyrus)

0x0A.description: Feedback from Bucyrus park brake
0x0A.name: Actual Park Brake State (Bucyrus)

DESC=Node Hours:HEX_MASK=1:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Stopped:1=Running
DESC=Pulsing Hours:HEX_MASK=2:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Stopped:1=Running

0x00.description:
bit field representing each of the hours counters 
bit0 "Node Hrs", bit1 "Pulsing Hrs", bit2 "Key Hrs", bit3 "Trac Hrs", bit4 "Pump Hrs", bit5 "PS Hrs", bit6 "Work Hrs"
The main use is to the tell CANopen display if an icon should be displayed for a particular activity
0x00.name: Running indicators

0x01.description: 1% / bit
0x01.name: BDI remaining charge

0x00.description: If set to 1, the Master will put itself and all the Slaves into pre-operational. The system is only allowed to enter operational when this is set to 0.

__meta__.name: Node Indication

0x00.name: Number of slaves

__meta__.description: Parameters to configure line contactor dropout function
__meta__.name: Line Contactor Dropout Parameters

0x01.description: 0 = Disabled, 1 = Enabled
0x01.name: Line contactor drop out

0x02.description: 1s/bit
0x02.name: Line contactor drop out timer

0x03.description:
Used to enable/disable line contactor features:
Bit 0 - Disable line contactor welded test. PMAC variants only.
0x03.name: Line contactor options

0x04.description: Target rate of charge of capacitors before line contactor is closed. A lower target causes capacitors to charge to a higher voltage, reducing inrush current, but increase start up time.
0x04.name: Line contactor final rate of charge

__meta__.description: Parameters to configure core line contactor control operation
__meta__.name: Line Contactor Control Parameters

0x01.description: Line contactor will not open unless all motors that are protected by the contactor are below this speed.
0x01.name: Line contactor open speed

0x02.description: Line contactor will not open unless all motors that are protected by the contactor are below this speed.
0x02.name: Line contactor close speed

0x03.bit_splitting:
DESC=Left traction motor:HEX_MASK=1:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Protected:1=Not protected
DESC=Right traction motor:HEX_MASK=2:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Protected:1=Not protected
DESC=Pump motor:HEX_MASK=4:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Protected:1=Not protected
DESC=Power steer motor:HEX_MASK=8:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Protected:1=Not protected
0x03.description:
Used to identify motors that are configured, but not protected by a line contactor.
Bit 0: Left traction motor
Bit 1: Right traction motor
Bit 2: Pump motor
Bit 3: Power steer motor
0x03.name: Motors not protected by line contactor

0x04.description: Line contactor will not close if the difference between Vcap and Vbat is greater than this.
0x04.name: Maximum line contactor arcing voltage

0x05.description: Line contactor will not close unless Vcap is above this level.
0x05.name: Minimum capacitor charge level

0x00.description: EMCY telegrams from Sevcon nodes include the fault ID in the manufacturer specific bytes. The fault ID gives the fault severity. Non-sevcon nodes will not supply this information so any EMCY message received from these nodes will be given the severity level defined in this object. Severity levels are: 0 - None, 1 - Warning, 2 - Drive Inhibit, 3 - Severe, 4 - Very severe and 5 - Return to base.
0x00.name: Assumed fault severity level for EMCY received from non-sevcon nodes.

__meta__.description: Parameters to configure alarm buzzer operation
__meta__.name: Alarm Buzzer Configuration

0x01.bit_splitting:
DESC=Forward:HEX_MASK=1:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=No:1=Yes
DESC=Reverse:HEX_MASK=2:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=No:1=Yes

0x01.description:
Set bit to enable buzzer source:
Bit 0 - Forward.
Bit 1 - Reverse.
Bit 2 - Controlled Roll Off.
Bit 3 - Fault.
Bit 4 - BDI Low.
Bit 5 - Inching Forward.
Bit 6 - Inching Reverse.
Bit 7 - Electrolyte Low.
0x01.name: Alarm buzzer source enable flags.

0x02.description: 0.1s/bit. Buzzer on-time when vehicle is moving forward.
0x02.name: Forward Cadence On Time

0x03.description: 0.1s/bit. Buzzer off-time when vehicle is moving forward.
0x03.name: Forward Cadence Off Time

0x04.description: 0.1s/bit. Buzzer on-time when vehicle is moving backwards.
0x04.name: Reverse Cadence On Time

0x05.description: 0.1s/bit. Buzzer off-time when vehicle is moving backwards.
0x05.name: Reverse Cadence Off Time

0x06.description: 0.1s/bit. Buzzer on-time when a fault is active.
0x06.name: Fault Cadence On Time

0x07.description: 0.1s/bit. Buzzer off-time when a fault is active.
0x07.name: Fault Cadence Off Time

0x08.description: 0.1s/bit. Buzzer on-time when vehicle is rolling away.
0x08.name: Controlled Roll Off Cadence On Time

0x09.description: 0.1s/bit. Buzzer off-time when vehicle is rolling away.
0x09.name: Controlled Roll Off Cadence Off Time

0x0A.description: 0.1s/bit. Buzzer on-time when a fault is active.
0x0A.name: BDI low Cadence On Time

0x0B.description: 0.1s/bit. Buzzer off-time when a fault is active.
0x0B.name: BDI low Cadence Off Time

0x0C.description: 0.1s/bit. Buzzer on-time when vehicle is inching forward.
0x0C.name: Inch Forward Cadence On Time

0x0D.description: 0.1s/bit. Buzzer off-time when vehicle is inching forward.
0x0D.name: Inch Forward Cadence Off Time

0x0E.description: 0.1s/bit. Buzzer on-time when vehicle is inching forward.
0x0E.name: Inch Reverse Cadence On Time

0x0F.description: 0.1s/bit. Buzzer off-time when vehicle is inching forward.
0x0F.name: Inch Reverse Cadence Off Time

0x10.description: 0.1s/bit. Buzzer on-time when Battery Electrolyte is low.
0x10.name: Electrolyte low Cadence On Time

0x11.description: 0.1s/bit. Buzzer off-time when Battery Electrolyte is low.

__meta__.description: Parameters to configure service alert.
__meta__.name: Servicing

0x01.description: This automatically resets the service alert by setting the Next Service Due hours to the current hours + Service Interval. Alternatively, the Next Service Due hours can be updated manually.
0x01.name: Reset (write to reset next service time)

0x02.description:
Bits are:
0 - Enable (set to enable service alert).
1 - Active Contactor Drive output (2405h)
2 - Indicate service on controller LED (15FF).
0x02.name: Flags

0x03.description:
Used to select the source hours counter to use for service:
00h - Device key hours.
01h - Device pulsing hours.
80h - Vehicle key hours.
81h - Vehicle traction hours
82h - Vehicle pump hours
83h - Vehicle power steer hours
84h - Vehicle work hours
0x03.name: Source Hours Counter

0x04.description: 1 hour / bit. This is the time before the next service is due.
0x04.name: Time between services (Service Interval)

0x05.description: 1 hour / bit. The hours when the next service will be flagged. This can be set automatically to the source hours + interval by writing to subindex 1.
0x05.name: Next Service Due time

0x01.name: Cutback range for low traction discharge current

0x02.description: Traction drive torque will be cut back linearly if available charge current drops above this level. Set to zero to disable this function.
0x02.name: Cutback range for low traction charge current

0x00.description: Torque / Speed mode (0) or Closed Loop Speed Control (1).
0x00.name: Traction control mode (Torque/speed mode (0) or closed loop speed control (1))

0x00.description: 0.1s/bit
0x00.name: Hill hold delay

0x00.description: 0.1s/bit
0x00.name: Seat Switch delay

0x01.description: Time between vehicle stopping (or entering hill hold or rolloff if configured) and the electrobrake being applied. Set this value to zero if you do not want the electrobrake to be applied automatically when the vehicle stops.
0x01.name: Electromechanical brake apply delay

0x02.description: Time between electrobrake being released and drive being allowed. Set this if the electrobrake takes some time to physically release. Set to zero if you do not want a delay.
0x02.name: Electromechanical brake release delay

0x03.description: Used to select if the brake should remain applied until the motors are outputting torque or if it can release as soon as required.
0x03.name: Electromechanical brake control

0x04.description: Minimum current required to be seen in the electrobrake to validate that the doesn’t have a wiring fault.  If the current has not built up to the minimum level in 500ms then tracapp sets a drive inhibit  fault.  A value of 0 disables this feature.
0x04.name: Electromechanical brake min current W/O limit

0x06.description: Minimum speed required that the vehicle must stay below in order that the electrobrake engages for the duration of the ebrake closure delay.  If the speed is speed is seen to be greater whilst the ebrake timer is counting (i.e. the vehicle has been seen to stop) the ebrake doesn’t close.  Set to zero for normal operation i.e. apply brake regardless.
0x06.name: Min speed to apply brake

0x01.bit_splitting:
DESC=Proportional Braking:HEX_MASK=1:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=No:1=Yes
DESC=Directional Throttle:HEX_MASK=2:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=No:1=Yes
DESC=Speed Limit Mode:HEX_MASK=4:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Proportional to throttle:1=Fixed at maximum
DESC=Braking Directional Throttle:HEX_MASK=8:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=No:1=Yes
DESC=Reverse Speed Limit Encoding:HEX_MASK=10:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=No:1=Yes
DESC=Handbrake Fault:HEX_MASK=20:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Enabled:1=Disabled
DESC=Proportional Speed Limit in Braking:HEX_MASK=40:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Enabled:1=Disabled
DESC=Independent Drive Profiles (some builds only):HEX_MASK=80:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Disabled:1=Enabled
0x01.description:
Bit 0 - Direction braking proportional to throttle (0 = Not proportional, 1 = Proportional).
Bit 1 - Directional Throttle enable (0 = Non-directional throttle, 1 = Directional throttle).
Bit 2 - Proportional Speed Limit disable (0 = speed limit proportional to throttle, 1 = speed limit fixed)
Bit 3 - Braking Direction Throttle enable (0 = Directional throttle controls drive torque only, 1 = Directional throttle controls drive and brake torque)
Bit 4 - Reverse speed limit encoding (0 = No encoding (default), 1 = Encoding active)
Bit 5 - Enable/disable handbrake fault.
Bit 6 - Disable/enable Proportional speed limit during braking (0 - Speed limit proportional to throttle in drive and brake, 1 = Speed limit proportional to throttle in drive only)
Bit 7 - Independent drive profiles (some builds only)  (0 = Generic Gen4, 1 = Only active profile is looked at, if none selected Baseline is used)
0x01.name: Throttle Flags

0x02.description: 0 = User Defined (sub-indices 12-17), 1 = Linear, 2 = Curved, 3 = Crawl
0x02.name: Throttle input characteristic

0x04.description: In range -32767 to 32767, where +/-32767 = Maximum demand in forward / reverse
0x04.name: Throttle Start Value 1

0x07.description: As above
0x07.name: Throttle Start Voltage 2

0x08.description: As above
0x08.name: Throttle Start Value 2

0x09.description: As above
0x09.name: Throttle End Voltage 2

0x0A.description: As above
0x0A.name: Throttle End Value 2

0x0B.description: Voltage is normalized 0 - 65535
0x0B.name: User Defined Char Pt 1 Voltage

0x0C.description: Value is normalized 0 - 65535
0x0C.name: User Defined Char Pt 1 Value

0x0D.description: As above
0x0D.name: User Defined Char Pt 2 Voltage

0x0E.description: As above
0x0E.name: User Defined Char Pt 2 Value

0x10.description: As above
0x10.name: User Defined Char Pt 3 Value

0x11.name: Dual Output Throttle Effective Start Voltage

0x12.description: As above
0x12.name: Dual Output Throttle Effective Start Value

0x13.name: Dual Output Throttle Effective End Voltage

0x14.name: Dual Output Throttle Effective End Value

0x15.description: For dual track throttle the parameter represents the maximum difference in final value that the two calculated throttle outputs report.  If the new parameter is 0 then the software defaults to the legacy behavior and applies a hardcoded of  5%.   For standard linear throttle configurations the value refers to a percentage of the maximum throttle voltage parameter.  E.g. if the parameter is set to 10% on a 0.5V(0%) – 4.5V(100%) configuration, the algorithm will fault if the throttle voltage exceeds (4.5+(4.5*0.1))) = 4.95V.  Conversely for a  9.0V(0%) – 1V(0%) accelerator configuration a  (max)  throttle limit of  30% will cause a fault if the voltage drops below 1-(1*0.3)=0.7V.  If  the parameter is set to zero then the legacy behavior of 0.5V is applied to either scenario.
0x15.name: Throttle fault level

0x16.bit_splitting:
DESC=Latching direction bits to prevent rollback:HEX_MASK=1:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Disabled:1=Enabled
DESC=Seat latch for regen settings:HEX_MASK=2:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Enabled:1=Disabled
DESC=Brake feathering during seat regen:HEX_MASK=4:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Enabled:1=Disabled
DESC=Disable Motor Low Voltage warning:HEX_MASK=8:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Off:1=On
DESC=Opposing speed limit set to max (D8 / HVLP):HEX_MASK=10:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Disabled:1=Enabled
DESC=Non CANOpen Footbrake option:HEX_MASK=20:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Disabled:1=Enabled
0x16.description:
Bit 0 - Direction bits in controlword latch and don't return to neutral in some states to prevent roll-back - when pressing the throttle briefly on an incline (FV only (gen4) / D8 / HVLP)
Bit 1 - seat switch latch for seat regen settings 
Bit 2 - Brake feathering during seat regen
Bit 3 - Disable Motor Low Voltage warning
Bit 4 - Opposing speed limit set to max (D8 / HVLP)
Bit 5 - Non CANOpen Footbrake Option (0 = Disabled, 1 = Enabled)
0x16.name: Throttle Flags 2

__meta__.description: Enable/disable general pump features.
__meta__.name: General Pump Features

0x01.bit_splitting:
DESC=BDI Inhibit:HEX_MASK=1:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Ignored:1=Inhibits pump
DESC=Drive Enable:HEX_MASK=2:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Ignored:1=Disables pump
DESC=Seat Switch:HEX_MASK=4:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Ignored:1=Disables pump
DESC=Line Contactor:HEX_MASK=8:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Ignored:1=Disables pump
DESC=Compensated Power Steer:HEX_MASK=10:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Off:1=On
DESC=Minimum speed without trigger:HEX_MASK=20:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Disabled:1=Enabled
DESC=Pump to stop on low battery:HEX_MASK=40:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Disabled:1=Enabled
0x01.description:
Set bit to enable feature:
Bit 0 - Low BDI inhibits pump.
Bit 1 - Drive enable switch disables pump.
Bit 2 - Seat switch disables pump (after seat delay (0x2902) has expired).
Bit 3 - Ignore Line Contactor. Pump is allowed to run regardless of the line contactor state. Useful if the pump provides power steering.
Bit 4 - Include Power Steer target velocity
Bit 5 - Minimum speed without trigger (gives minimum speed regardless of trigger if true)
Bit 6 - Pump to stop on low battery
0x01.name: Pump Flags

0x02.description: Factor by which pump speed demand will reduce when pump pressure switch is closed
0x02.name: Pump pressure switch cutback strength

0x03.description: Time between pump pressure switch closing and cutback activating
0x03.name: Pump pressure switch cutback delay

0x01.description: 1s/bit

0x02.description:
Each bit defines a trigger:
Bit 0: Vehicle moving (with Encoder) / Power applied to motor(without Encoder).
Bit 1: FS1 switch
Bit 2: Direction selected.
Bit 3: Seat switch
Bit 4: Footbrake
Bit 5: INCH switches

__meta__.description: Allows the power steering speed to be reduced at higher vehicle speeds to stiffen the steering and give more stability. The power steering speed is reduced linearly as the speed approaches the maximum assist speed. The cutback is constant above this speed,
__meta__.name: Variable Assist Power Steering

0x02.description: Power steering speed (0x2B01,1) is scaled back by this factor (/32767) as speed approaches the maximum assist speed.
0x02.name: Power Steering Assist Cutback Factor

0x02.name: Battery Electrolyte Debounce Delay(obsolete)

0x01.name: Enable charging

0x02.description: Minimum speed required for charging to start, if speed falls below this limit charging stops
0x02.name: Minimum Speed

0x03.description: Maximum speed allowed for charging to start, if speed rises above this limit charging stops
0x03.name: Maximum Speed

0x04.description: Initial phase constant charging current level
0x04.name: Bulk Charging Current

0x05.description: Required voltage battery must reach to enter second (completion) phase
0x05.name: Completion Voltage

0x06.description: The above voltage is held until the current required to maintain this voltage falls to this level.  After which the final (equalization) phase starts
0x06.name: Completion phase minimum current

0x07.description: The above current is held until the voltage rises to this level
0x07.name: Equalization maximum voltage

0x08.description: Then the battery is left to discharge to this level and is held here until a timeout occurs (half the time to complete phase 1 and 2)
0x08.name: Equalization minimum voltage

0x01.description: Voltage in 12.4
0x01.name: Pt 1 Voltage

0x02.name: Pt 1 Voltage Cutback Gain

__meta__.description: The Im/Lm saturation map is used to calculate a dynamic value for gamma in the motor control, used when the motor is operating above its rated point.
__meta__.name: Im/Lm Saturation Map (for auto-config additional motor)

0x01.description: Mag current in 12.4 A(RMS)
0x01.name: Pt 1 Mag Current

0x02.description: Mag inductance in 4.12 H
0x02.name: Pt 1 Mag Inductance

__meta__.description: Used to configure the temperature measuring analog input.
__meta__.name: Motor Temperature Sensor (for auto-config additional motor)

0x01.description:
Sets thermistor mode. Can be:
0 - None. Analog input can be used for general purpose.
1 - PTC thermistor.
2 - Switch.
3 - PTC Thermistor and switch
0x01.name: Mode

0x02.description: PTC only. This is the voltage at the input when the temperature is 100 degrees C.
0x02.name: High Temperature Voltage

0x03.description: PTC only. This is the voltage at the input when the temperature is 0 degrees C.
0x03.name: Low Temperature Voltage

0x04.description: Indicates the digital input to use for the motor temperature switch input. FFh indicates there is no switch fitted. 0-7 maps to bits 0-7 in 6800,1; 8-15 maps to bits 0-7 in 6800,2; etc.
0x04.name: Switch source

__meta__.description: Used for encoder configuration parameters.
__meta__.name: Encoder Configuration (for auto-config additional motor)

0x01.description:
0 = Current Sink (e.g. Thalheim) or Open-Collector
1 = Voltage Driver (e.g. SKF)
0x01.name: Encoder Pull Up

0x02.description: Sets encoder polarity. Setting bit 6 (velocity polarity) to 0 multiplies encoder speed by 1. Setting to 1 multiplies encoder speed by -1. Bit 7 (position polarity) is not used and bits 0-5 are reserved.
0x02.name: Encoder Polarity

0x04.name: Motor revolutions per second
0x04.tooltips: Increments per second is the same as encoder pulses per revolution if motor revolutions per second equals 1

0x06.name: Maximum motor speed (for auto-config additional motor)

0x07.name: Maximum acceleration rate (for auto-config additional motor)

0x08.description: Maximum rate of change of torque. Torque ramping is handled by the Master, so this is only a safety limit. Object resolution is 1000ths of Motor Rated Torque (6076h) / s. TODO - Need an appropriate value for this.
0x08.name: Torque slope (for auto-config additional motor)

0x09.description: Defines the current cutback curve
0x09.name: Cutback aggression

0x0A.description: Used to limit torque demands under cutback scenarios
0x0A.name: Motor continuous torque

0x01.bit_splitting:
DESC=Proportional Braking:HEX_MASK=1:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=No:1=Yes
DESC=Directional Throttle:HEX_MASK=2:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=No:1=Yes
DESC=Proportional Speed Limit:HEX_MASK=4:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=No:1=Yes

0x01.description:
Bit 0 - Direction braking proportional to throttle (0 = Not proportional, 1 = Proportional).
Bit 1 - Directional Throttle enable (0 = Non-directional throttle, 1 = Directional throttle).
Bit 2 - Proportional Speed Limit disable (0 = speed limit proportional to throttle, 1 = speed limit fixed)
Bit 3 - Braking Direction Throttle enable (0 = Directional throttle controls drive torque only, 1 = Directional throttle controls drive and brake torque)
Bit 4 - Reverse speed limit encoding (0 = No encoding (default), 1 = Encoding active)

DESC=Proportional Speed Limit:HEX_MASK=4:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=No:1=Yes
DESC=Braking Directional Throttle:HEX_MASK=8:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=No:1=Yes
DESC=Reverse Speed Limit Encoding:HEX_MASK=10:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=No:1=Yes
0x01.description:
Bit 0 - Direction braking proportional to throttle (0 = Not proportional, 1 = Proportional).
Bit 1 - Directional Throttle enable (0 = Non-directional throttle, 1 = Directional throttle).
Bit 2 - Proportional Speed Limit disable (0 = speed limit proportional to throttle, 1 = speed limit fixed)
Bit 3 - Braking Direction Throttle enable (0 = Directional throttle controls drive torque only, 1 = Directional throttle controls drive and brake torque)
Bit 4 - Reverse speed limit encoding (0 = No encoding (default), 1 = Encoding active)
0x01.name: Throttle Flags

0x02.description: 0 = User Defined (sub-indices 12-17), 1 = Linear, 2 = Curved, 3 = Crawl
0x02.name: Throttle input characteristic

0x03.description: Same units as 2200h
0x03.name: Throttle Start Voltage 1

0x04.description: In range -32767 to 32767, where +/-32767 = Maximum demand in forward / reverse
0x04.name: Throttle Start Value 1

0x07.description: As above
0x07.name: Throttle Start Voltage 2

0x08.description: As above
0x08.name: Throttle Start Value 2

0x09.description: As above
0x09.name: Throttle End Voltage 2

0x0A.description: As above
0x0A.name: Throttle End Value 2

0x0B.description: Voltage is normalized 0 - 65535
0x0B.name: User Defined Char Pt 1 Voltage

0x0C.description: Value is normalized 0 - 65535
0x0C.name: User Defined Char Pt 1 Value

0x0D.description: As above
0x0D.name: User Defined Char Pt 2 Voltage

0x0E.description: As above
0x0E.name: User Defined Char Pt 2 Value

0x10.description: As above
0x10.name: User Defined Char Pt 3 Value

__meta__.description: Enable/disable general pump features.
__meta__.name: General Pump Features  (Configuration 0)

0x01.bit_splitting:
DESC=BDI Inhibit:HEX_MASK=1:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Ignored:1=Inhibits pump
DESC=Drive Enable:HEX_MASK=2:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Ignored:1=Disables pump
DESC=Seat Switch:HEX_MASK=4:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Ignored:1=Disables pump
DESC=Line Contactor:HEX_MASK=8:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Ignored:1=Disables pump
DESC=Compensated Power Steer:HEX_MASK=10:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Off:1=On
DESC=Minimum speed without trigger:HEX_MASK=20:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Disabled:1=Enabled
DESC=Pump to stop on low battery:HEX_MASK=40:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Disabled:1=Enabled
0x01.description:
Set bit to enable feature:
Bit 0 - Low BDI inhibits pump.
Bit 1 - Drive enable switch disables pump.
Bit 2 - Seat switch disables pump (after seat delay (0x2902) has expired).
Bit 3 - Ignore Line Contactor. Pump is allowed to run regardless of the line contactor state. Useful if the pump provides power steering.
Bit 4 - Include Power Steer target velocity
Bit 5 - Minimum speed without trigger (gives minimum speed regardless of trigger if true)
Bit 6 - Pump to stop on low battery
0x01.name: Pump Flags

0x02.description: Factor by which pump speed demand will reduce when pump pressure switch is closed
0x02.name: Pump pressure switch cutback strength

0x03.description: Time between pump pressure switch closing and cutback activating
0x03.name: Pump pressure switch cutback delay

0x01.description: Voltage in 12.4
0x01.name: Pt 1 Voltage

0x02.name: Pt 1 Voltage Cutback Gain

__meta__.description: Used to configure the temperature measuring analog input.
__meta__.name: Motor Temperature Sensor (Configuration 0)

0x01.description:
Sets thermistor mode. Can be:
0 - None. Analog input can be used for general purpose.
1 - PTC thermistor.
2 - Switch.
3 - PTC Thermistor and switch
0x01.name: Mode

0x02.description: PTC only. This is the voltage at the input when the temperature is 100 degrees C.
0x02.name: High Temperature Voltage

0x03.description: PTC only. This is the voltage at the input when the temperature is 0 degrees C.
0x03.name: Low Temperature Voltage

0x04.description: Indicates the digital input to use for the motor temperature switch input. FFh indicates there is no switch fitted. 0-7 maps to bits 0-7 in 6800,1; 8-15 maps to bits 0-7 in 6800,2; etc.
0x04.name: Switch source

0x05.description: Allows user defined thermistor range (using high and low temperature voltages in subs 2 and 3) or predefined thermistor characteristic.
0x05.name: PTC type

__meta__.description: Used for encoder configuration parameters.

0x01.description:
0 = Current Sink (e.g. Thalheim) or Open-Collector
1 = Voltage Driver (e.g. SKF)

0x03.description:
0 = AB encoder only (induction motors)
1 = AB and UVW (PMAC)
2 = UVW only (PMAC)
3 = AB and UVW (inverted) (PMAC)
4 = UVW only (inverted) (PMAC)
0x03.name: Encoder Type

0x04.description: 0 to 180 degrees, value entered in 8.8 fmt
0x04.name: UVW Offset

0x01.description: Write to this object to commit changes to all values in 4641h and the lookup tables (4610h to 4613h).
0x01.name: Commit

0x02.description: 16.0 fixed point integer
0x02.name: Maximum Stator Current (Is_max)

0x03.description: 8.8 fixed point integer
0x03.name: Minimum Magnetizing Current (Im_min)

0x04.description: 10.6 fixed point integer
0x04.name: Maximum Magnetizing/Direct Current (Id_max)

0x07.description: 16.0 fixed point integer
0x07.name: Rated Stator Current

0x08.description: 4.12 fixed point integer (TODO - No longer required)
0x08.name: Rotor Resistance (Rr)

0x09.description: 0.16 fixed point integer
0x09.name: Magnetizing Inductance (Lm)

0x0A.description: -4.20 fixed point integer
0x0A.name: Stator Leakage Inductance (Lls)

0x0B.description: -4.20 fixed point integer
0x0B.name: Rotor Leakage Inductance (Llr)

0x0C.description: 12.4 fixed point integer
0x0C.name: Nominal battery voltage

0x0D.description: 1.15 fixed point integer
0x0D.name: Current control proportional gain (Kp)

0x0E.description: 1.15 fixed point integer
0x0E.name: Torque control integral gain

0x10.description: 0.16 fixed point integer
0x10.name: Reactive Gain Boost

0x12.description: Used for permanent magnet motors only, represents the back emf of the rotor with respect to speed.  This can then be translated directly into Iq demand (1.15 format)
0x12.name: Voltage Constant (Ke)

0x20.description: 8.8 fixed point (set to 0x0000 to use auto calculated value)
0x20.name: Ls/Ls'

0x25.description: 16.0 fixed point integer. Above rated speed, this is the rate at which Id_ref will increase having been reduced due to field weakening. Setting to 20000 effectively disables the ramp.
0x25.name: Id recovery rate

0x26.description: Value Ud_factor assumes when vehicle is travelling above 1000rpm (8.8 fixed point integer)
0x26.name: Ud stabilization factor (open loop slip)

0x27.description: Value Iq_factor is multiplied by at low slip values (8.8 fixed point integer)
0x27.name: Iq_min factor (open loop slip)

0x28.description: Value used to reduce vf ratio with slip (8.8 fixed point integer)
0x28.name: vf gain (open loop slip)

0x29.description: Stored estimation for Rs (4.12 fixed point integer)
0x29.name: Stored Rs estimation

0x2A.description: Maximum rate of change of Iq_ref
0x2A.name: Iq_ref slew rate

0x2B.description: Defines the minimum value that Ls can take when being calculated on line
0x2B.name: Percentage minimum allowed saturation of Ls

0x2C.description: Defines the maximum percentage of link voltage allowed on the d-axis
0x2C.name: Percentage maximum d-axis voltage

0x00.bit_splitting:
DESC=Encoder wire-off detection:HEX_MASK=1:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Enabled:1=Disabled
DESC=Disable software over current:HEX_MASK=2:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Enabled:1=Disabled
DESC=Use low speed gains below:HEX_MASK=4:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=200rpm:1=50rpm
DESC=Closed loop slip compensation:HEX_MASK=8:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Disabled:1=Enabled
DESC=Scale max torque by power limit:HEX_MASK=8:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Disabled:1=Enabled
DESC=Used fanned I2t cutback profile:HEX_MASK=8:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Disabled:1=Enabled
DESC=Allow drv/brk trq inhibit to 0 RPM:HEX_MASK=8:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Disabled:1=Enabled
0x00.description:
Used to disable/enable various features on the DSP
Bit 0: Disable encoder wire-off detection
Bit 1 : Disable software over current
Bit 2 : Use low speed gains
Bit 3 : Enable closed loop slip compensation
Bit 4 : Scale max torque by power limit
Bit 5 : Used fanned I2t cutback profile
Bit 6 : Allow drv/brk trq inhibit to 0 RPM
0x00.name: Miscellaneous DSP configuration (Configuration 0)

DESC=Use low speed gains below:HEX_MASK=4:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=200rpm:1=50rpm
DESC=Closed loop slip compensation:HEX_MASK=8:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Disabled:1=Enabled
DESC=Scale max torque by power limit:HEX_MASK=8:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Disabled:1=Enabled
DESC=Used fanned I2t cutback profile:HEX_MASK=8:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Disabled:1=Enabled
DESC=Allow drv/brk trq inhibit to 0 RPM:HEX_MASK=8:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Disabled:1=Enabled
0x00.description:
Used to disable/enable various features on the DSP
Bit 0: Disable encoder wire-off detection
Bit 1 : Disable software over current
Bit 2 : Use low speed gains
Bit 3 : Enable closed loop slip compensation
Bit 4 : Scale max torque by power limit
Bit 5 : Used fanned I2t cutback profile
Bit 6 : Allow drv/brk trq inhibit to 0 RPM
0x00.name: Miscellaneous DSP configuration (Configuration 2)

0x00.description:
Used to disable/enable various features on the DSP
Bit 0: Disable encoder wire-off detection
Bit 1 : Disable software over current
Bit 2 : Use low speed gains
Bit 3 : Enable closed loop slip compensation
Bit 4 : Scale max torque by power limit
Bit 5 : Used fanned I2t cutback profile
Bit 6 : Allow drv/brk trq inhibit to 0 RPM

0x00.name: Torque slope (Configuration 1)

0x00.description: If the motor slip reaches this value, bits 13 (pv only) and 14 (pv & tq) in the statusword are set. Units are in Hz (24.8 fixed pt integer). NOTE: DSP402 specifies velocity units which is RPM in our system, but this is not an appropriate unit for slip.

0x00.name: Maximum Motor Slippage (Configuration 1)

__meta__.description: Used to control all the logs. At present there is only one control item, the global reset.
__meta__.name: Logging Control

0x01.description: Writing to this index will reset all the logs. This reset can be performed differently to the individual log reset items in that it also initializes the EEPROM for log usage. This will need to be done after new software has been programmed into the controller. Read back a zero indicates log has been reset
0x01.name: Reset (write to reset all logs)

__meta__.description: Used to monitor and configure the System FIFO
__meta__.name: System FIFO Control

0x01.description: Writing to this index will reset the System FIFO. Only items with a log level less than of equal to the current logged in user access level will be deleted.
0x01.name: Reset (write to reset system FIFO)

0x02.description: The current length of the System FIFO
0x02.name: Number of items in System FIFO

0x03.description: Each bit corresponds to a group. Setting a bit allows events in that group to be logged.
0x03.name: Event Group Filter (set bit to inhibit logging events from this group)

0x00.description: The item to be returned when reading the System FIFO Output object.
0x00.name: Select System FIFO item to read in 4102h

__meta__.description: Outputs the FIFO entry selected in the input object.
__meta__.name: System FIFO Output

0x01.description: 16-bit event ID
0x01.name: Event ID

0x02.description: Controller key hours when event was logged.
0x02.name: Hours

0x03.description: Controller key minutes / seconds when event was logged.
0x03.name: Minutes / Seconds

0x04.description: Each event can have up to 3 bytes of data logged with it.
0x04.name: Data Byte 1

0x03.name: Event Group Filter (set bit to inhibit logging events from this group)

DESC=Is max clamp:HEX_MASK=1:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Inactive:1=Active

DESC=DC link power limit:HEX_MASK=8:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Inactive:1=Active

DESC=Thermal cutback:HEX_MASK=14:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Inactive:1=Active
DESC=Speed limit:HEX_MASK=18:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Inactive:1=Active

__meta__.description: Used to configure the temperature measuring analog input.

0x01.description:
Sets thermistor mode. Can be:
0 - None. Analog input can be used for general purpose.
1 - PTC thermistor.
2 - Switch.
3 - PTC Thermistor and switch
4 - Remote

DESC=Invert Angle Direction:HEX_MASK=1:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:1=Enabled:0=Disabled
DESC=Invert Speed Direction:HEX_MASK=2:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:1=Enabled:0=Disabled
DESC=Encoder 5V pullup:HEX_MASK=4:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:1=Enabled:0=Disabled
DESC=Encoder auto-align test:HEX_MASK=8:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:1=Enabled:0=Disabled

0x0B.description:
Primary encoder controlword:
0x0001 flips angle direction
0x0002 flips speed direction
0x0004 Encoder 5V pullup (if present)
0x0008 Perform encoder auto-align test
Bits 4-7 (0x00f0) Adjustable speed filter

0x0C.bit_splitting:
DESC=Invert Angle Direction:HEX_MASK=1:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:1=Enabled:0=Disabled
DESC=Invert Speed Direction:HEX_MASK=2:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:1=Enabled:0=Disabled

0x0001 flips angle direction

0x0008 Perform encoder auto-align test
Bits 4-7 (0x00f0) Adjustable speed filter

0x0F.description:
Status of auto-alignment routine.
0x00FF: a progress counter: new angle produced at 16 (0x10),  resets at 4 if no motion detected.
0x0100 : flips every time a new result is present.
0xF000:  set if sequence is wrong (reverse it and repeat test).
0x0F.name: Auto-align status

0x20.name: Ls/Ls'

0x2A.description: Maximum rate of change of Iq_ref (16.0 fixed point)

0x2E.description: Above zero user-defines how D and Q Ki are rescaled when in low-frequency PWM (8.8 fixed point)
0x2E.name: Iq and Id Ki scaling factor for low-frequency PWM.

0x2F.description: Above zero activates magnetic gearbox encoder skew and defines breakout torque in Nm. (15.1 fixed point)
0x2F.name: Magnetic gearbox breakout torque

0x30.description: 4.12 fixed point. Compensates Rs to give rated magnetizing current when stationary. Set to zero to disable (used only for IM VF control)
0x30.name: Rs temperature compensation Kp gain (IM V/f)

0x31.description: 4.12 fixed point. Compensates Rs to give rated magnetizing current when stationary. Set to zero to disable (used only for IM VF control)
0x31.name: Rs temperature compensation Ki gain (IM V/f)

0x32.description: 4.12 fixed point. Limits the motor current by altering the applied frequency (used only for IM VF control)
0x32.name: V/f current limit Kp gain (IM V/f)

0x33.description: 0.16 fixed point. Limits the motor current by altering the applied frequency (used only for IM VF control)
0x33.name: V/f current limit Ki gain (IM V/f)

0x35.description: 16.0 Speed at which no gain scheduling is applied
0x35.name: Current Control Gain Scheduling High Speed

0x36.description: 4.12 Factor applied fully at speed 0x4641,52 and then ramped towards no factor applied (factor of 1) at the speed 0x4641,53. Must be enabled in 0x4650,2 bit 13.

__meta__.description: Configuration for DC motors
__meta__.name: DC Motor data (manufacturer specific)

0x01.description: 12.4 Max armature current (Amps) - default 350A - used to scale field-armature maps - can't exceed hardware rating or be less than 1A
0x01.name: Maximum armature current

0x02.description: 12.4 Max field current (Amps) - default 25A - used to scale field-armature maps - can't exceed hardware rating or be less than 1A. Used for DC-SEM only
0x02.name: Maximum field current (DC-SEM only)

0x03.description: s16.0 Field slew up rate (Amps/sec) - default 20,000A/s - used to limit rate of increase in field current reference. Used for DC-SEM only
0x03.name: Field current up slew rate limit (DC-SEM only)

0x04.description: s16.0 Field slew down rate (Amps/sec) - default -20,000A/sec - used to limit rate of decrease in field current reference. Used for DC-SEM only
0x04.name: Field current down slew rate limit (DC-SEM only)

0x05.description: 0.16 Mutual armature inductance (H) - default 0.00H - if set to zero will be calculated automatically at power on. Used for DC-SEM in closed loop modes only
0x05.name: Motor mutual armature inductance (DC-SEM only)

0x06.description: 4.12 Armature resistance (Ohm) - default 0.00Ohm - if set to a non-zero value this will be used to help determine direction if no directional motor encoder is configured. WARNING if this is set too high the wrong motor direction will be reported only use if required. It can be compensated with temperature using the parameters in 0x4621, 4-7
0x06.name: Motor armature resistance

0x07.description: 12.4 Battery voltage when fully charged (V) - default 48V - cant exceed hardware rating or be less than 1V - set this to the maximum expected battery voltage (fully charged with no load)
0x07.name: Fully charged battery voltage

0x08.description: 16.0 Maximum motor speed (RPM) - default 5000 - cant be zero - this should match the speeds in 0x6080 and the maximum speed in the driveability profiles 0x2920, 0x2921, 0x2922
0x08.name: Maximum speed

0x09.description: 4.12 Field current proportional gain - default 0.5. Used for DC-SEM only
0x09.name: Field current proportional gain (DC-SEM only)

0x0A.description: 4.12 Field current integral gain - default 0.05. Used for DC-SEM only
0x0A.name: Field current integral gain (DC-SEM only)

0x0B.description: 4.12 Armature current proportional gain - default 0.25
0x0B.name: Armature current proportional gain

0x0C.description: 4.12 Armature current integral gain - default 0.025
0x0C.name: Armature current integral gain

0x0D.description: 4.12 Minimum applied voltage to field (V), this should be set very small (sufficient voltage to give 1A field current), if set too high will cause current control faults, should only be used if roll off is enabled otherwise leave set at zero. Used for DC-SEM only
0x0D.name: Minimum applied field voltage (DC-SEM only)

0x0E.description: 2.14 Voltage constant - default 0V/rads - Represents the back emf of the rotor with respect to speed. Used for DC permanent magnet motors in closed loop modes only
0x0E.name: Voltage Constant (Ke) (DC-PM only)

0x0F.description: 16.0 Armature current trip level (1000A/sec) - default 2,000,000 A/sec - if this di/dt is exceeded 3 times in a second a fault will be set. This threshold can be increased if a low inductance motor is used.
0x0F.name: Armature di/dt trip level

0x00.bit_splitting:
DESC=Encoder wire-off detection:HEX_MASK=1:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Enabled:1=Disabled
DESC=Software overcurrent:HEX_MASK=2:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Enabled:1=Disabled

DESC=Scale max torque by power limit:HEX_MASK=10:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Disabled:1=Enabled
DESC=Gain Scheduling:HEX_MASK=20:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Disabled:1=Enabled
DESC=Allow drv/brk trq inhibit to 0 RPM:HEX_MASK=40:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Disabled:1=Enabled
DESC=Motor open circuit detection:HEX_MASK=80:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Disabled:1=Enabled
DESC=Speed dependent field weakening:HEX_MASK=100:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Disabled:1=Enabled
DESC=Motor fault state (PMAC only):HEX_MASK=200:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Open Circuit:1=Short Circuit
DESC=Bypass DC link discharge at power on (PMAC Only):HEX_MASK=400:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Discharge:1=Bypass
DESC=Use lookup tables for demand (IMPM PMAC only):HEX_MASK=800:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Disabled:1=Enabled
DESC=Switching freq allowed to change to - reduce thermal losses (PMAC) / control low field currents (DC-SEM):HEX_MASK=1000:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Yes:1=No
DESC=Motor Forward and Reverse Speed Limits:HEX_MASK=2000:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Applied in drive direction only:1=Equal and opposite
DESC=SinCos MinMax Tracking:HEX_MASK=8000:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Enabled:1=Disabled
0x00.description:
Used to disable/enable various features on the DSP
Bit 0: Disable encoder wire-off detection
Bit 1 : Disable software over current
Bit 2 : Use low speed gains
Bit 3 : Enable sin-cos encoder PLL filter (PMAC), Closed loop slip compensation (GPAC pump IM)
Bit 4 : Decouple Kp and Ki current control gains (PMAC, Size 2-6)
Bit 5 : Enable Gain Scheduling (PMAC only)
Bit 6 : Allow drv/brk trq inhibit to 0 RPM
Bit 7 : Enable motor open circuit detection(Nano only)
Bit 8: Enable speed dependent field weakening
Bit 9: Short/Open circuit motor during severe fault
Bit 10: Bypass DC link discharge
Bit 11 : Use lookup tables for demands
Bit 12 : Disable variable switching frequency (PMAC / DC-SEM), Enable bridge disable when spinning (IM FV)
Bit 13: Make fwd+rev speed limits equal and opposite
Bit 14: Not used
Bit 15: Disable SINCOS MINMAX Tracking
0x00.name: Miscellaneous DSP configuration (Gen4)

DESC=Motor Forward and Reverse Speed Limits:HEX_MASK=2000:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Applied in drive direction only:1=Equal and opposite

0x01.description:
Used to disable/enable various features on the DSP
Bit 0: Disable encoder wire-off detection
Bit 1 : Disable software over current
Bit 2 : Use low speed gains
Bit 3 : Enable sin-cos encoder PLL filter (PMAC), Closed loop slip compensation (GPAC pump IM)
Bit 4 : Decouple Kp and Ki current control gains (PMAC, Size 2-6)
Bit 5 : Enable Gain Scheduling (PMAC only)
Bit 6 : Allow drv/brk trq inhibit to 0 RPM
Bit 7 : Enable motor open circuit detection(Nano only)
Bit 8: Enable speed dependent field weakening
Bit 9: Short/Open circuit motor during severe fault
Bit 10: Bypass DC link discharge
Bit 11 : Use lookup tables for demands
Bit 12 : Disable variable switching frequency (PMAC / DC-SEM), Enable bridge disable when spinning (IM FV)

DESC=Armature PWM percentage mode (DC voltage mode only):HEX_MASK=1:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Disabled:1=Enabled

0x02.description:
Used to disable/enable various features on the DSP
Bit 0: Armature PWM percentage mode (DC-SEM / DC-PM voltage mode only 0x6060 = 5)
Bit 1: Torque demand generated from peak torque or power limit map output.

Bit 3: Disable capacitors charged check

Bit 5: Not used in this software
Bit 6: Not used in this software

Bit 8: Not used in this software
Bit 9: Not used in this software
Bit10: Not used in this software
Bit11: Not used in this software
Bit14: Allow PMAC voltage vector to move in all quadrants (PFC inverter only)

0x01.name: Proportional gain

0x02.bit_splitting: DESC=CANopen autoconfig status:HEX_MASK=3:MIN=0:MAX=2:NF=3:0=disabled(e2 default):1=is auxiliary (hardcoded settings):2=is supervisor (e2 default settings)
0x02.description: indicates which CANopen configuration is in effect.
0x02.name: Integral gain
0x02.tooltips: values other than "disabled" indicate that the system has automatically configured itself

0x03.description: indicates which  configuration is in effect.
0x03.name: Low Speed proportional gain

0x04.description: indicates size of tires fitted
0x04.name: Rollback integral gain

0x07.description: Determines how speed limit integral (in torque mode) is initialized when starting to limit. Integral is set equal to actual torque multiplied by this factor. Normally, integral is set equal to actual torque so this object is set to 1.0 (1000h). This object is not used in speed mode.
0x07.name: Integral initialization factor

0x08.description: Used to filter speed measurement passed to speed limit and speed control loop. When enabled it will bypass any existing filtering within hal_encoder to allow full control of the speed feedback and find a compromise between noise and lag. Setting to zero disables this filter and uses the default in hal_encoder (3Hz for sin-cos, 9Hz for UVW, none for AB). It is calculated P = e^(-0.005*2*pi*f_c) where f_c is the desired cut-off frequency (Hz). Calculation is P = e^(-0.001*2*pi*f_c) for HVLP and D8
0x08.name: Speed calculation filter pole

0x09.description: Allow user definable speed pre-limits. This object is not used in speed mode.
0x09.name: Pre-limit speed

0x0A.description: Maximum ramp down rate allowed for speed limit when attempting to ramp speed limit back to 0 RPM. Only applied to opposing speed limit. See 0x6083,0 for normal speed limit ramp rate.
0x0A.name: Maximum speed limit ramp down rate

0x0B.description: Speed at which low speed gains are applied. Gains are ramped to high speed gains as speed approaches high gains speed threshold.
0x0B.name: Low gains speed threshold

0x0C.description: Speed at which high speed gains are applied. Gains are ramped to low speed gains as speed approaches low gains speed threshold.
0x0C.name: High gains speed threshold

0x00.description:
Used to temporarily disable/enable various features on the DSP
Bit 0: Disable current control quality monitor (PMAC / DC only)
Bit 1: Force low-frequency PWM mode (DC-SEM and PMAC Gen4 size8/10 only)
Bit 2: Force field established to be TRUE (DC-SEM only)
Bit 3: Force motor short circuit tests to run (DC only) - for testing spurious di/dt trips
Bit 4: Not used in this software

Must be in the range 0.005PU to 1PU. Should also not give a field current less than 2A.
__meta__.name: Armature/Field maps (DC-SEM only) - Roll off PU Field current axis

0x00.bit_splitting:
DESC=Encoder wire-off detection:HEX_MASK=1:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Enabled:1=Disabled
DESC=Software overcurrent:HEX_MASK=2:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Enabled:1=Disabled
DESC=Use low speed gains below:HEX_MASK=4:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=200rpm:1=50rpm
DESC=Sin-cos PLL (PMAC) / Closed loop slip compensation (IM):HEX_MASK=8:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Disabled:1=Enabled
DESC=Scale max torque by power limit:HEX_MASK=10:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Disabled:1=Enabled
DESC=Gain Scheduling:HEX_MASK=20:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Disabled:1=Enabled
DESC=Allow drv/brk trq inhibit to 0 RPM:HEX_MASK=40:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Disabled:1=Enabled
DESC=Motor open circuit detection:HEX_MASK=80:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Disabled:1=Enabled
DESC=Speed dependent field weakening:HEX_MASK=100:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Disabled:1=Enabled
DESC=Motor fault state (PMAC only):HEX_MASK=200:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Open Circuit:1=Short Circuit
DESC=Bypass DC link discharge at power on (PMAC Only):HEX_MASK=400:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Discharge:1=Bypass
DESC=Use lookup tables for demand (IMPM PMAC only):HEX_MASK=800:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Disabled:1=Enabled
DESC=Switching freq allowed to change to - reduce thermal losses (PMAC) / control low field currents (DC-SEM):HEX_MASK=1000:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Yes:1=No
DESC=Motor Forward and Reverse Speed Limits:HEX_MASK=2000:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Applied in drive direction only:1=Equal and opposite
DESC=SinCos MinMax Tracking:HEX_MASK=8000:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Enabled:1=Disabled
0x00.description:
Used to disable/enable various features on the DSP
Bit 0: Disable encoder wire-off detection
Bit 1 : Disable software over current
Bit 2 : Use low speed gains
Bit 3 : Enable sin-cos encoder PLL filter (PMAC), Closed loop slip compensation (GPAC pump IM)
Bit 4 : Decouple Kp and Ki current control gains (PMAC, Size 2-6)
Bit 5 : Enable Gain Scheduling (PMAC only)
Bit 6 : Allow drv/brk trq inhibit to 0 RPM
Bit 7 : Enable motor open circuit detection(Nano only)
Bit 8: Enable speed dependent field weakening
Bit 9: Short/Open circuit motor during severe fault
Bit 10: Bypass DC link discharge
Bit 11 : Use lookup tables for demands
Bit 12 : Disable variable switching frequency (PMAC / DC-SEM), Enable bridge disable when spinning (IM FV)
Bit 13: Make fwd+rev speed limits equal and opposite
Bit 14: Not used
Bit 15: Disable SINCOS MINMAX Tracking
0x00.name: Miscellaneous DSP configuration

DESC=PWM Switching Frequency:HEX_MASK=1:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=8kHz:1=12kHz
DESC=Deadtime Compensation:HEX_MASK=2:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Enabled:1=Disabled

0x00.name: PWM Configuration
0x00.tooltips: Bits 2 and 3 engineering test only. Bit 3 = 6kHz, Bit 4 = alternative SVM switching.

0x01.description: Time out after which the bridge is disabled, this must be longer than the line contactor drop out time
0x01.name: Bridge disable time

0x02.description: This is the speed at which the bridge will disable if there is no torque demand
0x02.name: Bridge disable speed

0x03.description: Default 2000Hz (16.0) - must be smaller than the maximum below and in the range 2-12kHz
0x03.name: Minimum switching frequency

0x04.description: Default 8000Hz (16.0) - must be larger than the minimum above and in the range 2-12kHz
0x04.name: Maximum switching frequency

0x05.description:
Bit 0: Set to disable deadtime compensation
Bit 1: Set to disable bridge disable timeout / speed
Bit 2: Set to enable bus clamping modulation (reduce switching losses)
0x05.name: PWM configuration

0x06.description: The torque output level at which the bridge will disable if there is no torque/speed demand and the motor speed is below the threshold in sub-index 2. (default 1%)
0x06.name: Bridge disable torque output

0x01.bit_splitting:
DESC=Powerframe fault ride-through:HEX_MASK=1:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Disabled:1=Enabled
DESC=Overvoltage fault ride-through:HEX_MASK=2:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Disabled:1=Enabled
DESC=Software overcurrent fault ride-through:HEX_MASK=4:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Disabled:1=Enabled
DESC=Current control fault ride-through:HEX_MASK=8:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Disabled:1=Enabled
DESC=Encoder fault ride-through:HEX_MASK=10:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Disabled:1=Enabled
DESC=Allow encoder fault retry after x ms settle period:HEX_MASK=100:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=Disabled:1=Enabled
DESC=Encoder retry settle period time:HEX_MASK=200:MIN=0:MAX=1:NF=2:0=100ms:1=250ms
0x01.description:
Bits to enable fault ride through for various faults. 
Bit 0: Powerframe fault (hardware overcurrent)
Bit 1: Overvoltage fault
Bit 2: Software overcurrent fault
Bit 3: Current control fault
Bit 4: Encoder fault
Bit 8: Use a retry if encoder fault persists after 100/250ms rather than set a fault and shutdown.
Bit 9: Toggle 250/100ms for bit 8 (1 = 250ms)
0x01.name: Ride-through flags

0x02.description: Number of ride-throughs before a suppressed fault disables controller.
0x02.name: Retries

0x03.description: Amount of max power to remove on a fault event (0 = none).
0x03.name: Cutback amount

0x04.description: Minimum torque to cut back to (if zero is too low).
0x04.name: Minimum torque

0x05.description: Amount of max power to recover over time.
0x05.name: Recovery gradient

0x06.description: Specify a speed above which controller cannot re-enable. 0 = 80% default.
0x06.name: Re-enable speed

0x01.description: Node ID of the device.
0x01.name: Node ID

0x03.description: Node ID of the IO processor.
0x03.name: Node ID (IO processor)

0x00.description: Set to TRUE to enable CAN fault detection on standalone units. Units configured on multi-node systems always have CAN fault detection enabled.
0x00.name: CANbus fault detection on Standalone units

0x00.description:
Used to select an RPDO timeout fault scheme. There are 4 options:
0 - RPDO time out disabled
1 - RPDO time out sets a warning fault and activates driveability select 2 profile to limit vehicle speed.
2 - RPDO time out sets a drive inhibit fault. Vehicle enters neutral.
3 - RPDO time out sets a severe fault. Line contactor opens and motor stops immediately.
0x00.name: RPDO timeout scheme

__meta__.description: Used to setup the desired heatsink fan profile. Note that subindices 3..10 are only used if performance is set to User Defined.
__meta__.name: Heatsink Fans

0x01.description:
FAN Control performance. Can be set to:
0. User Defined (FAN profile setup using si 3..10)
1. Maximum Performance (Give maximum cooling)
2. General Purpose.
3. Minimal Noise (Reduced noise, but also less cooling).
0x01.name: Fan Performance selection

0x02.description: Temperature below which heatsink is deemed to be cold.
0x02.name: Cold Temperature Threshold

0x03.description: Temperature above which heatsink is deemed to be warm.
0x03.name: Warming Temperature Threshold

0x04.description: Temperature above which heatsink is deemed to be hot.
0x04.name: Hot Temperature Threshold

0x05.description: Temperature below which heatsink is deemed to be cool.
0x05.name: Cooling Temperature Threshold

0x06.description: If Cold Temperature Threshold is near room temperature, the heatsink temperature may never reach it. This provides a time out after which the heatsink state changes from Cooling to Cold
0x06.name: Cool Down Time (after this time, the heatsink is assumed to be cold)

0x07.description: Speed of fans when the heatsink is cold. Can be Off (0), Half Speed (1) or Full Speed (2).
0x07.name: Fan Speed when cold

0x08.description: Speed of fans when the heatsink is warming. States as above.
0x08.name: Fan Speed when warm

0x09.description: Speed of fans when the heatsink is hot. States as above.
0x09.name: Fan Speed when hot

0x0A.description: Speed of fans when the heatsink is cooling. States as above.
0x0A.name: Fan Speed when cooling down

0x0B.description: Heatsink Fan configuration. Can be None (0), Pulsed Feedback (1) or Digital Feedback (2). Pulsed feedback refers to Fans with encoder feedback. Digital feedback refers to Fans with stopped/moving signal.
0x0B.name: Fan Type

__meta__.description: Used to set up external fan output
__meta__.name: External heat sink fans

0x01.description: Analogue output used to drive fans. Set to zero to disable external fans.
0x01.name: External fan analogue output

0x02.description: External fans are turned on when heat sink temperature is above this value
0x02.name: External fan turn on temperature

0x03.description: External fans are turned off when heat sink temperature is below this value
0x03.name: External fan turn off temperature

0x04.description: Voltage required to drive external fans
0x04.name: Fan voltage

0x05.description: Fan Temperature Source. Set to 0 for controller heatsink temperature or 1 for motor thermistor temperature
0x05.name: Fan Temperature Source

__meta__.description: Used to set up Internal fan output
__meta__.name: Internal heat sink fans

0x01.description: Number of fans connected internally. Set to zero to disable internal fans.
0x01.name: Number of Internal fans

0x02.description: Internal fans are turned on when heat sink temperature is above this value
0x02.name: Internal fan turn on temperature

0x03.description: Internal fans are turned off when heat sink temperature is below this value
0x03.name: Internal fan turn off temperature

0x04.description: Type of feedback on fans
0x04.name: Fan feedback

0x05.description: speed below which the fan is considered to be faulty when running.
0x05.name: Fan Fault speed

0x06.description: if zero the fans will not operate above 15V keyswitch voltage
0x06.name: Fan keyswitch voltage protection

__meta__.description: Maximum rate of change of torque. Torque ramping is handled by the Master, so this is only a safety limit. Object resolution is Nm/sec

0x02.description: Maximum rate of change for decreasing torque. Default 1000 (full torque range in 100msec)
0x02.name: Maximum torque ramp down rate

__meta__.description: This factor is used to convert increments into incs/s. This is fixed at 1.

0x00.description: If the motor slip reaches this value, bits 13 (pv only) and 14 (pv & tq) in the statusword are set. Units are in Hz (24.8 fixed pt integer). NOTE: DSP402 specifies velocity units which is RPM in our system, but this is not an appropriate unit for slip.
0x00.name: Maximum Motor Slippage

0x02.description: 8.8 fmt

0x04.description: 8.8 fmt
0x04.name: Rollback integral gain

0x07.description: Determines how speed limit integral (in torque mode) is initialized when starting to limit. Integral is set equal to actual torque multiplied by this factor. Normally, integral is set equal to actual torque so this object is set to 1.0 (1000h). This object is not used in speed mode.
0x07.name: Integral initialization factor

0x00.description: This is the unformatted output from the encoder. In DSP402, the units are increments. They are converted to inc/s using Velocity Factor 2 (6096h). However, for simplicity, Velocity Factor 2 is fixed at 1, so these units are also inc/s.
0x00.name: Encoder count

0x00.description: This is the output from the pv trajectory generator. The pv trajectory generator only performs a safety function so this object will equal Target Velocity (60FFh) most of the time. Its units are RPM
0x00.name: Velocity demand
0x00.tooltips: Velocity demand after velocity slope applied

0x00.description: Actual velocity in RPM.
0x00.name: Velocity

0x00.description: Target Torque in 1/1000th's of Motor Rated Torque (6076h). Calculated by the Application Master.
0x00.name: Target torque

0x00.description: Maximum Torque (Tmax) in 1/1000th's of Motor Rated Torque (6076h).
0x00.name: Maximum torque

0x00.description: Output from the tq controller. Value is in 1/1000th's of Motor Rated Torque (6076h).

0x00.name: Peak torque

0x00.name: Torque

0x00.name: Current

0x00.description: Voltage in mV applied to power frame. Referred to as Capacitor Voltage in previous Sevcon controllers.
0x00.name: Capacitor Voltage

0x00.description: Sets encoder polarity. Setting bit 6 (velocity polarity) to 0 multiplies encoder speed by 1. Setting to 1 multiplies encoder speed by -1. Bit 7 (position polarity) is not used and bits 0-5 are reserved.
0x00.name: Encoder Polarity

0x00.description: Maximum motor speed (ωnom) in RPM. It is unsigned and applies in both directions.
0x00.name: Maximum motor speed

0x00.description: Maximum acceleration and deceleration rates. Acceleration and Deceleration is controlled by the Master, so this is only a safety limit. Object format is 24.8 fixed point, units are Hz (i.e. 1/s).
0x00.name: Maximum acceleration rate

0x00.description: Defines how motor speed is accelerated / decelerated to the target velocity. 0 = Linear characteristic. Options 1, 2 and 3 (sin2, jerk-free and limited) are not supported. Since only linear is supported, this object is RO.
0x00.name: Motion profile type

0x00.description: Maximum rate of change of torque. Torque ramping is handled by the Master, so this is only a safety limit. Object resolution is 1000ths of Motor Rated Torque (6076h) / s. TODO - Need an appropriate value for this.
0x00.name: Torque slope

0x00.description: Defines how torque is ramped to target. 0 = Linear. Option 1 (sin2) is not supported. Since only Linear is supported, this object is RO.
0x00.name: Torque profile type

0x00.description: Velocity units are in RPM.
0x00.name: Velocity notation index

0x00.description: Velocity units are in RPM.
0x00.name: Velocity dimension index

0x00.description: CANopen does not have any suitable units for acceleration. Define as having no units for now.
0x00.name: Acceleration notation index

0x00.description: CANopen does not have any suitable units for acceleration. Define as having no units for now.
0x00.name: Acceleration dimension index

__meta__.description: Used to set up encoder pulse per revolution. PPR = Incs/s / Rev/s. By setting Rev/s to 1, Incs/s can be used to set the PPR directly.
__meta__.name: Encoder resolution

0x02.name: Motor revolutions per second
0x02.tooltips: Increments per second is the same as encoder pulses per revolution if motor revolutions per second equals 1

__meta__.description: This factor is used to convert RPM into velocity units. However, in this system, velocity units are in RPM, so the factor is fixed at 1.
__meta__.name: Velocity factor 1

__meta__.description: This factor is used to convert increments into incs/s. This is fixed at 1.
__meta__.name: Velocity factor 2

0x00.description: If the motor slip reaches this value, bits 13 (pv only) and 14 (pv & tq) in the statusword are set. Units are in Hz (24.8 fixed pt integer). NOTE: DSP402 specifies velocity units which is RPM in our system, but this is not an appropriate unit for slip.
0x00.name: Maximum Motor Slippage

0x00.description: In RPM. Motor target velocity in pv mode.
0x00.name: Target speed
